nodes.label: Quality Deviation Investigation
nodes.description: When a batch fails any quality test, trace the deviation back through the manufacturing chain to identify root cause and scope of impact.

nodes.label: Lot Recall Execution Workflow
nodes.description: When a recall is initiated, trace affected lots through the distribution chain, identify current locations, and generate recall notifications.

nodes.label: Supplier Audit Trigger
nodes.description: When a supplier quality score drops below threshold or an FDA warning letter is issued, trigger on-site audit within 30 days.

nodes.label: API Stability Failure Batch Trace
nodes.description: Taught by Quality Director: When an API fails stability at month 18, immediately check all downstream batches within 60 days of the API lot date. Do not wait for month 24 data.
nodes.creator: people-quality-dir

nodes.label: Cold Chain Excursion Response
nodes.description: Mined from 45 excursion events: 78% of excursions exceeding 2 hours but under 4 hours have no product impact. Above 4 hours, quarantine immediately.
nodes.creator: people-sc-manager

nodes.label: FDA Inspection Preparation
nodes.description: Learned from correction: When preparing for FDA inspection, always have the last 3 CAPA closures for each product line ready. Inspectors always ask for them.
nodes.creator: people-quality-dir

nodes.label: Quality Director

nodes.description: Department level. Manages supplier relationships, logistics, and cold chain operations.

nodes.label: Quality Assurance
nodes.description: Manages quality standards, batch testing, CAPA, and regulatory inspections.

nodes.label: Supply Chain
nodes.description: Manages supplier logistics, cold chain, and distribution operations.

questions.question: Metformin API fails stability test at month 18. What is the exposure?
questions.answer: API lot #M-7823 failure affects 3 downstream tablet batches. Lot #T-903 is still at the plant - hold immediately. Lots #T-901 and #T-902 are in distribution (hospital network and retail pharmacy chain). Quality Director should initiate the Batch Trace protocol immediately per the 60-day rule. Cold chain is not a factor. API Manufacturer India's quality score is borderline - flag for next audit cycle.
reasoningSteps: Quality Deviation Investigation triggers on Metformin API stability failure.
Skill fires: "API Stability Failure Batch Trace" (Executive priority) - immediately check all downstream batches within 60 days of API lot date.
Entity traversal: Metformin API -> manufactured at plant -> downstream product batches.
Lot Recall Execution workflow evaluates: trace affected lots through distribution chain.
Skill fires: "Cold Chain Excursion Response" - checks transport logs. No excursions detected.
Supplier Audit Trigger evaluates: API supplier quality score checked.
People routed: Quality Director gets batch impact assessment. Supply Chain Manager gets distribution chain trace.

questions.question: Metformin Hydrochloride API fails a stability test. Which downstream batches, plants, and pharmacies are affected?
questions.answer: Metformin HCl API from API Manufacturer India is used in Metformin 500mg Tablets, which are manufactured at FDA Facility NJ and packaged at Packaging Line TX. The tablets are available at Hospital Pharmacy Mass General and Retail Pharmacy CVS. Track & Trace (DSCSA) serializes at the packaging line and tracks the product. Quality Management System monitors both manufacturing and packaging plants for deviation handling.
reasoningSteps: Metformin HCl API supplied by API Manufacturer India
Used in Metformin 500mg Tablets
Manufactured at FDA Facility NJ
Packaged and serialized at Packaging Line TX
DSCSA tracks product through distribution
Available at Mass General and CVS pharmacies
QMS monitors both plants for deviation handling

questions.question: If Cold Chain Logistics Inc reports a temperature excursion during Insulin Glargine transport, what's the impact?
questions.answer: Cold Chain Logistics transports Insulin Glargine 100U/mL, which is manufactured at Sterile Fill-Finish IN using Insulin Glargine Bulk from API Manufacturer India and Glass Vials from the vial supplier. The insulin is available at Specialty Pharmacy BioScript and Hospital Pharmacy Mass General. A temperature excursion may compromise the entire shipment - DSCSA tracking identifies affected serial numbers for targeted recall.
reasoningSteps: Cold Chain Logistics transports Insulin Glargine
Insulin manufactured at Sterile Fill-Finish IN
Uses Insulin Glargine Bulk from API Manufacturer India
Glass vials supplied to the fill-finish facility
Available at BioScript Specialty and Mass General pharmacies
DSCSA serial numbers enable targeted recall of affected units

questions.question: API Manufacturer India is our only API supplier. What products are at risk if they fail an FDA inspection?
questions.answer: API Manufacturer India supplies Metformin HCl API and Insulin Glargine Bulk - the active ingredients for two of our four products. They are registered with FDA CDER. If they fail inspection, Metformin Tablets (available at Mass General and CVS) and Insulin Glargine (available at BioScript and Mass General) are both at risk. No alternative API supplier exists in the current supply chain - this is a critical single-source vulnerability.
reasoningSteps: API Manufacturer India supplies Metformin HCl and Insulin Glargine Bulk
Registered with FDA CDER submission portal
Metformin Tablets available at Mass General and CVS
Insulin Glargine available at BioScript and Mass General
No alternative API supplier in the system
FDA inspection failure would halt both product lines
